qas.q: Which is the most effective mean to store PHI?
The HIPAA Privacy Officer is responsible for:
qas.a: Tracking who has access to PHI

qas.q: The HIPAA Security Officers are responsible for:
qas.a: Safeguarding all electronic patient health information

qas.q: Which are the five areas of DHHS has mandated each covered entity to address so that e-PHI is maintained securely?
qas.a: Organization requirements; policies, procedures, and documentation; technical safeguards; administrative safeguards; and physical safeguards.

qas.q: Reasonable physical safeguards for patient care areas include:
qas.a: Having monitors turned away from viewing by visitors.

qas.q: To insure minimum opportunity to access data, passwords:
qas.a: Should be changed every 90 days or sooner.

qas.q: Investigations of complaints of violations to the Security Rule are under the direction of the:
With the passage of HIPAA, large healthcare providers would be treated with faster service since their volumes of claims is larger than small rural providers.
Nursing notes are not considered PHI since they are not physician's notes and therefore are not protected by HIPAA law.
It is possible for a first name and zip code to be considered individually identifiable health information (IIHI)
In HIPAA usage, TPO stands for Treatment, Payment, and Optional Care.
Trading Partner agreements are only for electronic standard transactions.
One good requirement to ensure secure access control is to install automatic log off at each workstation.
HIPAA seeks to protect individual PHI and discloses that information only when it is in the best interest of the patient.
Prescriptions may only be picked up by the patient to protect the privacy of the individuals health information.
Faxing PHI is still permitted under HIPAA law.
All four parties on a health claim now have unique identifiers.
PHI stand for:
DHHS stands for:
qas.a: Department of Health and Human Services

qas.q: NPO stands for:
Privacy Rule covers disclosure of protected health information (PHI) in any form or media.
Only clinical staff need to understand HIPAA law.
The HIPAA Privacy Rules gives patients assurance that their personal health information will be treated the same no matter which state or organization receives their medical information
The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) have information on their website to help a HIPAA Security Officer know the required and addressable areas of securing e-PHI.
Only a serious security incident is to be documented and measures taken to limit further disclosure.

PHI (protected health information) is:
A. Any information that identifies an individual with a diagnosis
B. Health information created or received by a covered entity.
C. Health information related to a physical or mental condition
D. All of the above

Consent is defined by HIPPA is for:
A. Permission to reveal PHI for payment of services provided to patients.
B. Permission to reveal PHI for comprehensive treatment of patient.
C. Permission to reveal PHI for normal business operations of the providers facility.
D. All the above

If the HIPAA Office finds that a trading partner that has changed the formatting of a standard transaction, the office may report the partner to:
qas.a: The Office of E-Health Standards and Services

qas.q: If the a Office of HIPAA Standards finds noncompliance to the Transaction and Code Set Rule, they will expect to see a move toward compliance and improvement within:
Access privilege to protected health information is:
qas.a: What allows an individual to enter a computer system for an authorized purpose

qas.q: Responsibilities of the HIPAA Security Officer include:
qas.a: Developing and implementing policies and procedures for the facility

qas.q: Integrity of e-PHI requires confirmation that the data:
qas.a: Is accurate and has not been altered, lost, or destroyed in an unauthorized manner

Questions other people have asked about HIPAA law can be found by searching FAQ at:
American Health Information Management Association (AHIMA) has found that the problem with HIPAA Privacy Rule are mainly those that:
qas.a: Account for the release of PHI

qas.q: Medical Identity theft is:
qas.a: Obtaining an individual's SSN from the Internet to use to gain their money

qas.q: In keeping with the "minimum necessary" policy, an office may leave:
qas.a: Only the doctors office phone number on voicemail.

qas.q: When patients "opt-out" of the facility directory, it means:
qas.a: Their name will not be posted outside the room where they are located

qas.a: Center of Medicare and Medicaid Services

qas.q: Fraud and abuse investigation of HIPAA Privacy Rule is under the direction of:
Acronym for Public Law 104-91
Which is not a responsibility of a HIPAA Officer?
qas.a: Safeguarding the security of clinical records

qas.q: PHI has been defined in HIPAA by:
qas.a: CMS (Centers of Medicare and Medicaid Services)
